title: Listening - Part 4: Learning to Cook Professionally
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
topic: five people discussing why they took an intensive professional cooking course

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’d always been a decent home cook, but [21]I decided to take the course because I was planning to open my own small bistro and wanted to ensure I had the technical skills to back up my passion[/21]. I wanted to be credible. [26]The most rewarding aspect has been the incredible boost in my time-management skills[/26]. Learning how to prep and cook for forty people at once requires a level of organization I never had before. It’s made me much more efficient in all areas of my life, even my morning routine is now streamlined! || Speaker 2: I’m a food blogger, and [22]I took the course primarily to gain a deeper understanding of the science behind cooking so I could write more authoritative articles[/22]. I wanted to be more than just a reviewer. However, [27]the biggest challenge was the high-pressure, almost military-style environment of a professional kitchen[/27]. I’m used to the slow, aesthetic pace of a home studio, so having a chef-instructor shouting at me for being ten seconds late with a sauce was a massive shock to the system. It was very stressful. || Speaker 3: I recently retired and [23]I took the course because I wanted a new challenge that would prevent me from becoming bored and stagnant[/23]. I wanted to keep learning. [28]What I didn't expect was the profound sense of physical satisfaction I get from working with my hands all day[/28]. After years in an office, there’s something wonderful about the tactile nature of kneading dough or filleting fish. It’s given me a new appreciation for manual work and a sense of accomplishment I hadn't felt in years. || Speaker 4: I’d been working as a server for years, and [24]I took the course because I wanted to move into the kitchen and double my earning potential[/24]. It was a career move. [29]The most difficult hurdle has been the physical toll it takes on my back and legs[/29]. Standing for twelve hours in a hot kitchen is a whole different level of tired. I’ve had to invest in very expensive shoes just to survive the week. It’s a grueling job that I definitely underestimated. || Speaker 5: I’d always struggled with low self-esteem, so [25]I took the course because I wanted to master a complex, tangible skill that would give me a sense of pride[/25]. I wanted to be 'the person who can cook'. [30]The most rewarding outcome has been the wonderful social circle I’ve built with my fellow students[/30]. We’re a diverse group, but we bonded over the shared trauma of burnt sauces and failed soufflés. It’s given me a whole new community of friends who support each other's culinary dreams and meet up every month to try a new restaurant together.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21–25, choose from the list (A–H) what primarily motivated each speaker to take the course.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 wanted technical skills to open his own bistro.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 wanted a deeper understanding of food science for her blog.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 wanted a new challenge to stay active and avoid boredom in retirement.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 wanted to move into the kitchen to increase her earning potential.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to master a skill to build her self-esteem and pride.

options.C: to fulfill a requirement for a job
options.D: to impress a specific social group
options.F: to save money on their own meals
options.G: to prepare for a future business venture

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26–30, choose from the list (A–H) what each speaker mentions as an outcome of the course.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 mentions a massive boost in his time-management skills.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 found the high-pressure, military-style kitchen atmosphere a shock.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 found physical satisfaction in working with his hands.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 mentions the physical toll standing for 12 hours takes on her back and legs.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 highlights the social circle and community she built with fellow students.

options.A: a change in their long-term career goal
options.C: an increase in their physical flexibility
options.D: a noticeable improvement in their efficiency
options.E: the discovery of a hidden creative talent
options.F: the physical strain of the environment
options.G: the difficulty of the teaching style
options.H: the value of the social support they found
